Imports

types, interactive/prompts

Types

CmdArg = ref object
  kind*: CmdLineKind
  dataType*: CmdArgValueType ## The type of the argument, used for parsing and validation at runtime
  name*: string              ## The name of the argument, used to reference it in the command's code block
  description*: string       ## A description of the argument that will be shown in the help message
  isOptional*: bool ## When an argument is optional, it means that the user
                    ## doesn't have to provide it when executing the command
The CmdArg object represents an argument of a command, it contains all the information about the argument, such as its name, type and whether it's optional or not, used for parsing and validation at runtime 
Command = ref object
  case
  of cmdCommand:
    description*: string     ## A description of the command that will be shown in the help message
  else:
    nil
The Command object represents a command in the CLI application, it contains all the information about the command, such as its name, description, arguments and the code block that will be executed when the command is called. 
CommandType = enum
  cmdCommand, cmdGroup, cmdSeparator
The type of the command, used for parsing and validation at runtime 
Kapsis = ref object
  author*, version*, license*, description*: string

Macros

macro initKapsis(stmtNodes: untyped)

Initializes a Kapsis application by parsing the commands.

Kapsis will try to collect metadata from the provided statements, if not provided it will try to collect it from the .nimble file.

Templates

template initApp(stmtNodes: untyped)
Alias for initKapsis, you can use either initKapsis or initApp to initialize your Kapsis application 
template initCLI(stmtNodes: untyped)
Alias for initKapsis, you can use either initKapsis or initCLI to initialize your Kapsis application 
template initCLIApplication(stmtNodes: untyped)
Alias for initKapsis, you can use either initKapsis or initCLIApplcation to initialize your Kapsis application

Exports
